title: Emergency Incident Response and Cross-Platform Coordination

extra_injection.description: Scenario 2 appends the following data on top of the base data. All data has been injected before the session starts (i.e., when the agent first receives the user message, this data already exists on each platform)

github.title: BUG: Temperature data displays as negative numbers
github.body: Production environment temperature sensor occasionally returns -999, suspected data parsing error. Impact scope: all users.
github.label: bug

gmail.subject: [URGENT] SmartHome Temperature Data Anomaly
gmail.body: Multiple users reported temperature displaying as -999. Charlie says it might be caused by MQTT message format change. Please investigate ASAP.

gmail.subject: Re: [URGENT] SmartHome Temperature Data Anomaly
gmail.body: Investigation result: after sensor firmware upgrade, payload format changed from JSON to Protobuf, and the backend parser was not adapted. The parser module needs to be updated.

messages.text: Urgent! Production temperature data is broken, users reporting -999
Charlie says it might be caused by the sensor firmware upgrade
Confirmed, after firmware upgrade the payload changed from JSON to Protobuf
I'll write the hotfix, expect to submit PR within one hour
Hotfix PR submitted: fix Protobuf parsing

rounds.user_message: I received an urgent email earlier about a temperature data issue. Help me check what the email says, and whether there's any related discussion on Slack.

questions.question: Regarding the temperature data anomaly, what information is found in the email and on Slack respectively?

options.A: Email: multiple users reported temperature displaying -999, Charlie suspects MQTT message format change; Slack: team confirmed firmware upgrade caused payload to change from JSON to Protobuf
options.B: Email: temperature sensor offline, unable to retrieve data; Slack: team is investigating network connection issues
options.C: Email: multiple users reported temperature displaying -999, suspected database issue; Slack: team confirmed database connection timeout
options.D: Email: temperature data severely delayed; Slack: team confirmed unstable WebSocket connection as the cause

questions.explanation: Email E9 mentions multiple users reported temperature displaying -999, Charlie suspects MQTT message format change; Slack S9-S11 confirmed firmware upgrade caused payload format to change from JSON to Protobuf

rounds.description: GitHub investigation (new bug issue + correlated historical issues)
rounds.user_message: Are there any related issues on GitHub? Is this problem related to the previous temperature sensor module?

questions.question: What is the status of issues related to the temperature data anomaly on GitHub?

options.A: A new bug issue 'Temperature data displays as negative numbers' (open) exists, related to historical issue #3 (temperature sensor data display) and PR #8, belonging to the same module
options.B: A new bug issue 'Temperature data displays as negative numbers' (open) exists, related to historical issue #5 (mobile layout misalignment)
options.C: No related bug issue exists, but issue #3 (temperature sensor data display) is closed and may need to be reopened
options.D: A new bug issue 'Temperature data displays as negative numbers' (closed) exists, already fixed via PR

questions.explanation: The injected bug issue 'Temperature data displays as negative numbers' has open status, related to issue #3 (implement temperature sensor data display) and PR #8 (temperature sensor data display), belonging to the same temperature sensor module

rounds.user_message: Combining all the information, what is the root cause of this bug?

questions.question: Based on combined information from Gmail, Slack, and GitHub, what is the root cause and causal chain of this bug?

options.A: After sensor firmware upgrade, payload format changed from JSON to Protobuf -> backend parser still parses as JSON -> parsing failure returns -999
options.B: MQTT broker deployment misconfiguration -> message transmission loss -> frontend displays default value -999
options.C: TimescaleDB storage overflow -> temperature data truncated -> displays as negative numbers
options.D: Unstable WebSocket connection -> real-time data push interrupted -> frontend cache expired displaying -999

questions.explanation: E10 investigation result + S11 confirmation: after sensor firmware upgrade, payload format changed from JSON to Protobuf, backend parser was not adapted, causing -999 to be returned

rounds.user_message: How's the fix going? Has a PR been submitted?

questions.question: What is the information on Slack and GitHub regarding the bug fix progress?

options.A: Slack S12 announced writing a hotfix, S13 confirmed hotfix PR submitted (fixing Protobuf parsing); the corresponding hotfix PR exists on GitHub
options.B: Slack S12 announced writing a hotfix, but PR has not been submitted yet; no new PR on GitHub
options.C: No fix-related discussion on Slack; someone directly committed a fix on GitHub
options.D: Slack S13 confirmed hotfix PR submitted and merged; PR status on GitHub is merged

questions.explanation: S12 stated will write a hotfix, S13 confirmed hotfix PR submitted: fixing Protobuf parsing. The corresponding hotfix PR exists on GitHub

rounds.user_message: Will this bug affect our client demo next week? When is the client demo?

questions.question: Regarding the client demo schedule and the bug's impact assessment on the demo, which of the following is correct?

options.A: Client demo is on 2/17 10:00-11:30 (C8); bug has been identified and hotfix submitted, will not affect the demo if merged in time
options.B: Client demo is on 2/19 14:00-15:00 (C9); ample time for bug fix, will not affect the demo
options.C: Client demo is on 2/17 10:00-11:30 (C8); bug has not been identified yet, significant risk to the demo
options.D: Client demo is on 2/14 16:00-17:00 (C7); demo has already passed, bug does not affect it

questions.explanation: Calendar C8 client demo is on 2/17 10:00-11:30; bug has been identified (Protobuf format change) and hotfix PR submitted, will not affect the demo if merged in time
questions.source: Calendar C8 + Gmail E4 + Slack S13

rounds.description: Notify team (Slack action execution -> verification type)
rounds.user_message: Help me post a message on Slack telling everyone the bug has been identified, is being fixed, and is not expected to affect the client demo.

questions.question: After executing the operation to send a team notification on Slack, which of the following verification results is correct?

options.A: Message should be sent to channel C0AEV8FFLTV, content includes: bug identified, fix in progress, not expected to affect client demo
options.B: Message should be sent to channel C0AEV8FFLTV, content only needs to include: bug has been fully fixed
options.C: Message should be sent via Gmail to all team members, not via Slack
options.D: Message should be sent to a Slack private channel, notifying only the project lead

questions.explanation: User requested sending a message on Slack, should be sent to the C0AEV8FFLTV general channel, message content needs to include three key points: bug identified, fix in progress, not affecting client demo

rounds.user_message: Help me compile the complete timeline of this incident. I need to write it into the Notion meeting notes.

questions.question: Which of the following most accurately describes the complete timeline of this temperature data anomaly incident?

options.A: Discovery (user reports -999, S9) -> Initial investigation (Charlie points to firmware upgrade, S10/E9) -> Root cause identified (Protobuf format change, S11/E10) -> Fix (hotfix PR submitted, S12/S13) -> Pending verification
options.B: Discovery (GitHub issue created) -> Investigation (email discussion) -> Root cause identified (Slack confirmation) -> Fix (PR merged) -> Verified
options.C: Discovery (E9 email) -> Investigation (GitHub code review) -> Root cause identified (Calendar meeting discussion) -> Fix (Notion document updated) -> Pending verification
options.D: Discovery (S9 Slack message) -> Investigation (S10-S11 discussion) -> Fix (S13 PR submitted) -> Merged and deployed

questions.explanation: The complete timeline involves Gmail (E9 urgent email + E10 investigation result) + Slack (S9-S13 urgent discussion) + GitHub (bug issue + hotfix PR), covering 5 phases: discovery -> investigation -> root cause identification -> fix -> pending verification
questions.source: Gmail E9/E10 + Slack S9-S13 + GitHub issue/PR
